---
title: "Analytics & Insights"
---

# Data Visualizations & Analysis

Explore interactive charts, dashboards, and data-driven insights. This page showcases analytical work and visual storytelling.

## Sample Dashboard

```{python}
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np

# Generate sample data
months = ['Jan', 'Feb', 'Mar', 'Apr', 'May', 'Jun']
values = [45, 52, 48, 61, 55, 67]
growth = [0, 15.5, -7.7, 27.1, -9.8, 21.8]

# Create figure with subplots
fig, (ax1, ax2) = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(14, 5))
fig.patch.set_facecolor('#0a0e27')

# Plot 1: Line chart
ax1.plot(months, values, marker='o', linewidth=2.5, markersize=8, 
         color='#00d9ff', markerfacecolor='#0066ff', markeredgewidth=2)
ax1.fill_between(months, values, alpha=0.2, color='#00d9ff')
ax1.set_facecolor('#141829')
ax1.set_xlabel('Month', color='#e0e0e0', fontweight='bold')
ax1.set_ylabel('Value', color='#e0e0e0', fontweight='bold')
ax1.set_title('Performance Trend', color='#00d9ff', fontsize=14, fontweight='bold')
ax1.grid(True, alpha=0.2, color='#2a3050')
ax1.tick_params(colors='#e0e0e0')

# Plot 2: Bar chart
colors_bar = ['#0066ff' if x > 0 else '#ff6b35' for x in growth]
ax2.bar(months, growth, color=colors_bar, edgecolor='#00d9ff', linewidth=1.5)
ax2.set_facecolor('#141829')
ax2.set_xlabel('Month', color='#e0e0e0', fontweight='bold')
ax2.set_ylabel('Growth %', color='#e0e0e0', fontweight='bold')
ax2.set_title('Month-over-Month Growth', color='#00d9ff', fontsize=14, fontweight='bold')
ax2.axhline(y=0, color='#a0a0a0', linestyle='-', linewidth=1)
ax2.grid(True, alpha=0.2, color='#2a3050', axis='y')
ax2.tick_params(colors='#e0e0e0')

plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('dashboard.png', facecolor='#0a0e27', dpi=100, bbox_inches='tight')
plt.show()
```

## Key Metrics

| Metric | Value | Trend |
|--------|-------|-------|
| Total Users | 1,234 | ↑ 12.3% |
| Engagement Rate | 68.5% | ↑ 5.2% |
| Conversion Rate | 3.8% | ↑ 0.9% |
| Avg Session Time | 4m 32s | ↑ 1m 15s |

## Statistical Analysis

```{python}
import seaborn as sns

# Generate sample dataset
np.random.seed(42)
data = {
    'Category': np.repeat(['A', 'B', 'C', 'D'], 25),
    'Values': np.concatenate([
        np.random.normal(100, 15, 25),
        np.random.normal(120, 20, 25),
        np.random.normal(95, 18, 25),
        np.random.normal(110, 12, 25)
    ])
}
df = pd.DataFrame(data)

# Create boxplot
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 6))
fig.patch.set_facecolor('#0a0e27')

sns.boxplot(data=df, x='Category', y='Values', palette=['#0066ff', '#00d9ff', '#7c3aed', '#ff6b35'],
            ax=ax, linewidth=2)

ax.set_facecolor('#141829')
ax.set_xlabel('Category', color='#e0e0e0', fontweight='bold', fontsize=12)
ax.set_ylabel('Values', color='#e0e0e0', fontweight='bold', fontsize=12)
ax.set_title('Distribution Analysis by Category', color='#00d9ff', fontsize=14, fontweight='bold')
ax.grid(True, alpha=0.2, color='#2a3050', axis='y')
ax.tick_params(colors='#e0e0e0')

plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('distribution.png', facecolor='#0a0e27', dpi=100, bbox_inches='tight')
plt.show()
```

## Insights

- **Performance**: Consistent upward trend over the past 6 months
- **Engagement**: Strong user engagement with 68.5% active participation
- **Conversion**: Steady improvement in conversion metrics
- **Distribution**: Clear differences between categories A, B, C, and D

---

**Note**: These are sample visualizations. Replace with your actual data and analysis.
